data.title: PayPal adds new board director
data.description:
• PayPal Holdings (NASDAQ:PYPL) (NEOE:PYPL:CA) announced on Monday that Joy Chik has joined the payment company's board of directors.
  • Chik serves as Microsoft's president of identity and network access, and is responsible for leading the company's security business, Microsoft Entra.
  • "PayPal (NASDAQ:PYPL) is aiming to radically change commerce over the next decade by increasing our velocity of innovation and accelerating our integration of artificial intelligence across our product suite and the global enterprise," said CEO Alex Chriss.
  • "Joy is an expert in technology and AI, and her counsel as a new member of the Board will help us harness cutting-edge technology as we deliver transformative solutions and personalized experiences to our hundreds of millions of customers worldwide," added Chriss.

data.title: State-owned companies were top greenhouse gas emitters in 2023, analysis finds
data.description:
More than half of the world's greenhouse gas emissions in 2023 were linked to just 36 companies, according to the Carbon Majors database by U.K.-based think tank InfluenceMap, with 16 of the top 20 emitters being state-owned.

State-run companies contributed 52% of global emissions in 2023. The top five state-owned emitters - Saudi Aramco (ARMCO), Coal India, China's CHN Energy and Jinneng Group, and National Iranian Oil - were responsible for 17.4% of global emissions in 2023.

The top five investor-owned emitters - ExxonMobil (NYSE:XOM), Chevron (NYSE:CVX), Shell (NYSE:SHEL), TotalEnergies (NYSE:TTE), and BP (NYSE:BP) - accounted for 4.9% of global emissions in 2023.

The analysis "reveals that, despite global climate commitments, a small group of the world's largest fossil fuel producers are significantly increasing production and emissions," Emmett Connaire, senior analyst at InfluenceMap, said.

Coal remained the largest source of emissions in 2023, contributing 41.1% of emissions in the database. Cement producers notched the largest relative increase at 6.5%, reflecting expanding production, while natural gas emissions declined by 3.7% and oil remained stable with a 0.3% increase.

data.title: ServiceNow to acquire Moveworks for $2.85B in cash and stock
data.description:
Update: Adds deal confirmation

ServiceNow (NYSE:NOW) has confirmed its acquisition of Mountain View, California-based artificial intelligence firm Moveworks for $2.85 billion in cash and stock.

The transaction is expected to close in the second half of 2025, subject to customary regulatory approvals and closing conditions. 

The acquisition will combine ServiceNow’s (NYSE:NOW) agentic AI and automation capabilities with Moveworks’ front-end AI assistant and enterprise search technology, enabling ServiceNow to accelerate its AI-powered solutions to improve customer interactions.

“With the acquisition of Moveworks, ServiceNow will take another giant leap forward in agentic AI-powered business transformation,”  said Amit Zavery, president, chief operating officer, and chief product officer at ServiceNow

Created in 2016, Moveworks offers an agentic AI Assistant that automates support across the entire enterprise operations. Its solution is used by 350+ large enterprises, including Palo Alto Networks, Siemens, Toyota, and Unilever.

The business surpassed $100M annual recurring revenue in September last year and has raised $315M in funding at a $2.1B valuation from investors such as Alkeon, Bain, Iconiq, Kleiner Perkins, Lightspeed, Sapphire, and Tiger Global.

Under the leadership of CEO Bill McDermott, ServiceNow has been integrating generative AI capabilities into its offerings. Before joining ServiceNow, McDermott served as the CEO of software giant SAP SE, where he spearheaded several major acquisitions.

"AI is fueling a top to bottom re-ordering of the enterprise technology landscape," McDermott said during the software firm’s latest earnings release.

Shares of ServiceNow (NYSE:NOW) were down over 3% premarket and have fallen nearly 20% year-to-date.

data.title: North American Construction reschedules Q4 report due to effects of Cyclone Alfred
data.description:
North American Construction Group (NYSE:NOA) said Monday it will reschedule the release of its Q4 financial results and conference call to March 19 after markets close, citing the unforeseen impacts of Cyclone Alfred in Australia's Queensland region.

North American Construction (NYSE:NOA) said it was required to reschedule the release to allow more time to complete the year-end reporting processes within its Heavy Equipment Australia segment, and incremental verification of parts and supply inventories is necessary due to first-year SOX reporting requirements, all within this segment which was previously acquired by the company in October 1 2023.

The company said the effects of Cyclone Alfred have included stormwater flooding, power outages, public service disruption, road closures and restrictions on domestic travel which have substantially increased the time and effort required to travel to and access mine sites in the region to verify certain parts and supply inventories.

data.title: China-based SunCar to develop insurance management platform for EV maker Leapmotor
data.description:
• China-based auto e-Insurance services provider SunCar Technology Group (NASDAQ:SDA) announced on Monday the bagging of a contract to develop an insurance management platform for the electric vehicle manufacturer, Leapmotor.
  • The contract expands SunCar's existing partnership with Leapmotor from providing traditional insurance agency services to core technology infrastructure.
  • The new platform is expected to centralize Leapmotor's insurance operations by automating policy issuance and renewal, enabling digital claims processing, coordinating post-accident services, and integrating with the company's customer app.

data.title: Glenstar begins trading on the OTCQB
data.description:
Glenstar Ventures (GSTRF) Monday said that the OTC Markets Group has approved the trading of its common shares on the OTCQB Venture Market.

The company's common shares commenced trading on the OTCQB under the symbol "GSTRF" as of the opening of the market on March 6, 2025.

data.title: Citi sticks to bullish outlook, targeting 6,500 for S&P 500 despite recent volatility
data.description:
Citi recently reaffirmed its bullish outlook on the broader market, even as Wall Street has experienced declines in recent weeks. The global investment firm is sticking to its year-end 2025 target of 6,500 for the S&P 500 (SP500) , a forecast based on an anticipated $270 in earnings per share for the index. 

Citi expressed strong confidence in the fundamental growth trajectory of the market, highlighting an expected broadening of earnings growth across sectors. However, the firm also cautioned that a period of heightened volatility is likely ahead for the bull market. 

As of now, the S&P 500 stands at 5,770. For Citi’s target of 6,500 to materialize, the index would need to climb 12.6% from its current level by the end of 2025.

This comes after a rough week for the S&P 500, which dipped to its lowest level since September 18, reaching a near six-month low as it tagged 5,666.29 on Friday.

When positioning in this uncertain environment, Citi recommends a "barbell strategy," blending growth-focused investments to navigate macroeconomic challenges with cyclicals that can provide stability against valuation and inflation risks. 

For investors looking to track the S&P 500’s movements, there are several index ETFs and mutual funds worth considering. Some options include:

S&P 500 Tracking Funds: (MUTF:FXAIX), (MUTF:VFIAX), (MUTF:VFFSX), (MUTF:SWPPX), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS) and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

data.title: Trevi Therapeutics meets main goal in chronic coughing treatment trial, stock surges
data.description:
Trevi Therapeutics (NASDAQ:TRVI) stock jumped about 40% premarket after its mid-stage trial for Haduvio, a potential treatment for chronic coughing, met main goal.

Haduvio, the company's investigational therapy for chronic coughing in patients with idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis and refractory chronic cough, significantly reduced coughing, with a 66% decrease in severe cases and a 68% reduction in moderate cases.

Overall, the company  said that 84% of patients taking Haduvio experienced at least a 30% drop in coughing, compared to just 29% of those on a placebo. 

The safety results in the trial were consistent with previous trials, with no serious side effects, Trevi added. The most common mild side effects were constipation, nausea, drowsiness, headache, dizziness, and fatigue.

data.title: Dave announces up to $50M class A stock buyback program
data.description:
• Neobank and fintech company Dave (NASDAQ:DAVE) announced on Monday an up to $50M class A share repurchase program.
  • DAVE shares were trading -2.77% pre-market at $81.05.
  • The company said the repurchase program does not have a fixed expiration date and may be executed from time to time based on market conditions, liquidity, and other strategic considerations.
  • The purchases may be made through a variety of methods, including open market transactions and privately negotiated transactions.
  • Dave expects to begin the buybacks imminently.

data.title: Flux Power appoints Krishna Vanka as CEO
data.description:
• Flux Power (NASDAQ:FLUX) said on Monday that it has appointed Krishna Vanka as Chief Executive Officer.
  • Vanka assumes the position on March 10, 2025, to replace Ron Dutt, whose planned retirement was previously announced.
  • Vanka was most recently the CEO of Fluence Digital, a part of Fluence Energy, a Nasdaq listed global market leader in energy storage.
  • Flux Power (FLUX) shares were up more than 4% in pre-market trading.
